title: Determine Area of Triangles
source_excerpt: Determine Area of Triangles Lesson launch

title: Do Now
source_excerpt: Do Now What shapes do you notice? Estimate which shape has the greater area.

title: Learning Target
source_excerpt: Learning Target Standard: 6.G.A.1 I can determine the area of a triangle using a related rectangle or parallelogram. Success Criteria I can identify the base and height. I can explain why the triangle area formula works…
mapping_note: Directly mapped to the listed session section(s).

title: Model
source_excerpt: Model Rectangle area review How does the rectangle help you reason about triangle area?
mapping_note: Directly mapped to the listed session section(s).

title: Guided Practice
source_excerpt: Guided Practice Triangle A has base 10 units and height 6 units. What is its area? Which measurement is the height?
mapping_note: Directly mapped to the listed session section(s).

title: Independent Practice
source_excerpt: Independent Practice Find the area of Triangle B with base 12 units and height 9 units. Show your work and label units.
mapping_note: Directly mapped to the listed session section(s).

title: Discussion
source_excerpt: Discussion Explain why doubling the triangle creates a parallelogram. What misconception should we watch for?
mapping_note: Directly mapped to the listed session section(s).

title: Exit Ticket
source_excerpt: Exit Ticket A triangle has area 24 square units and height 6 units. What is the base? Explain your reasoning.
